text: General maintenance teams keep shift notes concise, record equipment status, and escalate unresolved anomalies before handoff. Documentation uses plain language so the next operator can recover context without relying on tribal memory.

A reliable work log lists the observed condition, the action taken, the person responsible, and the expected follow-up date. Clear records reduce duplicated effort and help teams compare routine behavior against unusual events.

Safety reviews separate facts from recommendations. Reviewers confirm timestamps, check that measurements are reproducible, and avoid changing operating procedure until the evidence supports a durable pattern.
